mainHeading: ISI Certification & ISI Marking Certification in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The ISI mark is a certification mark for industrial products in India. It means, the product complies with the Indian quality and safety standards laid down by the BIS Act, 2016. ISI stands for Indian Standards Institute with ISI mark being compulsory for a number of products like electrical items, cement, packaged drinking water and others. Professionals Law helps manufacturers and importers obtain ISI Certification by facilitating product testing, compliance documentation and liaising with BIS officials.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: ISI Certification is is important for the safety of the public, reliability of the product and to comply with the law. ISI certification of the Indian Government is required for several products, being sold in Indian market. ‎Vlad Alexeev‎ There's national standards compliance involved in this which helps avoid shoddy or dangerous goods reaching consumers.

body.title: Mandatory for Various Products
body.description: ISI certification is compulsory for over 150 products. Without it, manufacturers cannot legally produce or sell items like electrical goods, gas cylinders and packaged water.

body.title: Builds Consumer Confidence
body.description: The ISI mark acts as a symbol of trust, assuring buyers of the product’s quality, safety and reliability.

body.title: Improves Market Access
body.description: Products with ISI certification are eligible for sale in government tenders and large institutional purchases, widening business opportunities.

body.title: Ensures Standardized Production
body.description: It promotes consistency in manufacturing processes and quality control in line with Indian standards.

body.title: Legal Protection Against Non-compliance
body.description: Avoids regulatory penalties and legal action for selling non-compliant products in regulated markets.

criteria: Manufacturers of products covered under Compulsory Certification Scheme of BIS.
The product must conform to relevant Indian Standard (IS) codes.
The applicant must own a manufacturing facility or have authorized access.
Testing of samples must be conducted in BIS-recognized labs.
Foreign manufacturers must appoint an Authorized Indian Representative (AIR).

documents: Product specifications and IS code reference
Copy of PAN, GST and IEC (if applicable)
Agreement with Indian Representative (for foreign applicants)

body.step: Product Evaluation and IS Code Selection
body.description: Professionals Law identifies the applicable Indian Standard Code for the product.
Preparation of BIS application with all mandatory declarations.
Samples are sent to BIS-approved laboratories for testing.
BIS conducts a factory audit to verify manufacturing process and quality control.
Upon successful compliance, ISI certificate is issued and permission is granted to use the ISI mark.
Periodic surveillance and renewal are conducted to retain the validity of the certification.

body.title: Penalties
body.description:
Selling or manufacturing products under mandatory ISI certification without a valid license can result in:
Fines up to ₹2 lakhs per offense
Imprisonment up to 2 years
Seizure of goods or cancellation of manufacturing license
Blacklisting from government procurement
Professionals Law helps clients avoid such risks by ensuring full regulatory compliance.
The ISI Certification process generally takes 30 to 90 days

body.question: What is ISI Certification?
body.answer: It is a quality certification issued by BIS indicating that a product complies with Indian standards.

body.question: Is ISI Certification mandatory for all products?
body.answer: No, it is mandatory only for products listed under the compulsory certification scheme. For others, it is voluntary.

body.question: Can foreign manufacturers obtain ISI certification?
body.answer: Yes, they must appoint an Authorized Indian Representative (AIR) and follow the same BIS procedures.

body.question: How long is the ISI certificate valid?
body.answer: Typically, it is valid for 1 to 2 years, with periodic renewals and surveillance audits.

body.question: Can one ISI certificate cover multiple products?
body.answer: No, each product and each factory location require a separate certification.

body.question: What happens if a certified product fails in quality?
body.answer: BIS may suspend or cancel the license and impose penalties.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help with lab testing?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law coordinates with BIS-approved labs and facilitates sample testing.

body.question: How is the ISI mark affixed?
body.answer: Once certified, the manufacturer must imprint the ISI logo along with the license number on the product and packaging.

body.question: Is renewal a complex process?
body.answer: No, with timely surveillance and continued compliance, Professionals Law ensures smooth and prompt renewal of ISI certification.
